title: Meet Sara
description: Listen to Sara introduce herself, then prove you were listening

_voiceScript.script: Volám sa Sara. Som z Talianska, ale bývam v Bratislave. Som mladá a nízka. Mám hnedé vlasy a zelené oči. Nemám rodinu tu — moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku. Mám mačku. Volá sa Luna. Pracujem tu od marca. Som šťastná tu na Slovensku.

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Volám sa Sara. Som z Talianska, ale bývam v Bratislave. Som mladá a nízka. Mám hnedé vlasy a zelené oči. Nemám rodinu tu — moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku. Mám mačku. Volá sa Luna. Pracujem tu od marca. Som šťastná tu na Slovensku.

exercises.instruction: Tap every word or expression you heard in the conversation.

exercises.conversationTranscriptSk: Volám sa Sara. Som z Talianska, ale bývam v Bratislave. Som mladá a nízka. Mám hnedé vlasy a zelené oči. Nemám rodinu tu — moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku. Mám mačku. Volá sa Luna. Pracujem tu od marca. Som šťastná tu na Slovensku.

questions.answerMeaning: Som z Talianska = I am from Italy. Same Som z... pattern as Marek's Som z Nitry — the country name changes but the structure is identical.

questions.questionEn: How does Sara describe her appearance?

answers.textEn: Young and short with brown hair and green eyes
Old and tall with black hair

questions.answerMeaning: Som mladá a nízka. Mám hnedé vlasy a zelené oči = I am young and short. I have brown hair and green eyes. Notice mladá and nízka use the female form — Marek said vysoký (male form).

questions.questionEn: Does Sara have family in Bratislava?

answers.textEn: Yes, her whole family lives there
No, her parents live in Italy
Yes, she has a sister there

questions.answerMeaning: Nemám rodinu tu — moji rodičia bývajú v Taliansku = I don't have family here — my parents live in Italy. Nemám = I don't have. The opposite of Mám from the Verbs lesson.

questions.questionEn: What pet does Sara have and what is it called?
questions.answerMeaning: Mám mačku. Volá sa Luna = I have a cat. She's called Luna. Same Volá sa... pattern as Marek's dog Rex — works for any pet's name.

reviewPair.english: I have a cat. She's called Luna.

questions.questionEn: Since when has Sara been working in Bratislava?
questions.answerMeaning: Pracujem tu od marca = I have been working here since March. Od + month — same pattern as Marek's od septembra. Different month, same structure.

reviewPair.english: I have been working here since March

questions.questionEn: What do Sara and Marek have in common based on what they said?

answers.textEn: They both live in Bratislava and are happy in Slovakia

questions.answerMeaning: Both said: Bývam v Bratislave and Som šťastný/šťastná tu na Slovensku. Notice šťastná (Sara, female form) vs šťastný (Marek, male form) — same feeling, different ending based on gender.

reviewPair.english: I am happy here in Slovakia

sentences.promptEn: I am from Italy, but I live in Bratislava.
I have brown hair and green eyes.
I am happy here in Slovakia.
